# Phase 10.0.13 Human Dignity Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, autonomous governance, APIs, background jobs, automated social-control systems, coercive governance automation systems, or execution authority.

## Objective

Preserve human dignity, rights continuity, anti-dehumanization safeguards, humane restraint, human agency, anti-authoritarian continuity, and anti-coercion governance after institutional self-correction doctrine.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional human-dignity doctrine.
- Anti-dehumanization governance philosophy.
- Constitutional rights-preservation standards.
- Humane-governance survivability architecture.
- Institutional respect-for-personhood doctrine.
- Anti-authoritarian continuity framework.
- Governance coercion-containment doctrine.
- Constitutional humane-restraint philosophy.
- Institutional human-agency survivability standards.
- Civilization dignity-preservation architecture.
- Phase 10.0.13 human dignity summary.

## Validation Checklist

- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.
- No dignity-preservation layer creates runtime authority.
- No rights review bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No humane-governance layer enables hidden execution escalation.
- No dignity framework weakens auditability.

## Review Notes

- Humane governance remains human-reviewed, auditable, non-runtime, non-operational, anti-coercive, and anti-authoritarian.
- Rights continuity preserves agency, dissent, consent, review, and personhood.
- Dignity doctrine rejects social-control systems and coercive automation.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, autonomous governance, automated social-control systems, coercive governance automation systems, or execution systems.
